summary: Backend-only verification completed for wc2026tracker deployment readiness. Frontend build passed and root API wrapper files are present/loadable, but all required public API contracts failed due to 404 on deployed endpoint.

critical.issue: Expected 200 with {ok:true}; got 404 (after Vercel redirect to trackwc2026.vercel.app).
Expected usage payload; got 404 (route not resolved to serverless handler).
Persistence flow blocked: GET returns 404 so Redis write/read cannot be validated.

action_items: Fix Vercel domain/project mapping so /api/* routes execute wc2026tracker handlers instead of returning 404.
Ensure REDIS_URL and STATS_API_KEY are configured in the same Vercel project/environment receiving traffic.
Re-run the new pytest file after deployment mapping/env fix to verify health/stats/results contracts.
critical_code_review_comments: Code-level wrappers are correct: /api/health.js, /api/stats.js, /api/results.js successfully re-export frontend handlers and load under Node.
Root cause appears deployment/routing-level, not handler syntax/build-level.
No /app/memory/test_credentials.md found; flagged per instruction (not blocking for these non-auth tests).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed backend-only tests against public URL with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://wc2026tracker.vercel.app. Build succeeds; wrappers exist/load. Public API routes still return 404 after redirect to trackwc2026.vercel.app. Start next run by validating Vercel project/domain linkage and env vars in target project.
rca of the issue: Repro: curl/pytest against /api/health, /api/stats?action=usage, /api/results on wc2026tracker.vercel.app -> 307 redirect to trackwc2026.vercel.app -> 404. This prevents handler execution and Redis/API-key validation. Mitigation: correct Vercel domain mapping to deployment containing these serverless endpoints, verify REDIS_URL/STATS_API_KEY in that deployment, then retest contracts.
